name: uv
context: Ultra-fast Python package and project manager written in Rust — replaces pip, pip-tools, virtualenv, poetry, and pyenv in a single tool
info: uv is Astral's Rust-based Python package and project manager that is 10-100x faster than pip. It provides a unified CLI for creating projects (uv init), managing dependencies (uv add/remove), resolving and locking (uv lock), syncing environments (uv sync), running commands (uv run), managing Python versions (uv python install), installing CLI tools globally (uv tool install / uvx), building packages (uv build), publishing to PyPI (uv publish), and a full pip-compatible interface (uv pip install/compile/sync). It uses a global cache to avoid redundant downloads, generates a cross-platform uv.lock lockfile, and integrates with pyproject.toml for all configuration. uv replaces the need for pip, pip-tools, virtualenv, venv, poetry, pyenv, pipx, and conda in most workflows.
triggers: create a new python project with uv
run a script in uv project
run a tool without installing it globally
body:
# uv — Fast Python Package Manager

uv is Astral's ultra-fast Python package and project manager, written in Rust. It replaces pip, pip-tools, virtualenv, poetry, and pyenv with a single, unified tool that is 10-100x faster.

## Installation

```bash
# Standalone installer (recommended)
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Or via pip
pip install uv

# Or via Homebrew
brew install uv
```

## Quick Start — Project Workflow

```bash
# Create a new project with pyproject.toml
uv init my-project
cd my-project

# Add dependencies
uv add requests httpx
uv add --dev pytest ruff mypy

# Sync environment to lockfile
uv sync

# Run commands in the project environment
uv run python main.py
uv run pytest
```

uv creates a `.venv` automatically, generates `uv.lock`, and resolves dependencies in seconds.

## Project Management

### Creating Projects

```bash
# New application project
uv init my-app

# New library project
uv init --lib my-lib

# Initialize in current directory
uv init
```

`uv init` creates `pyproject.toml`, `.python-version`, `README.md`, and a `src/` or `hello.py` scaffold.

### Dependency Management

```bash
# Add a dependency
uv add requests
uv add "httpx>=0.27"

# Add dev dependency
uv add --dev pytest pytest-asyncio

# Add optional dependency group
uv add --optional docs sphinx

# Remove a dependency
uv remove requests

# Update a dependency
uv lock --upgrade-package requests
```

### Locking and Syncing

```bash
# Resolve dependencies and write uv.lock
uv lock

# Sync .venv to match uv.lock exactly
uv sync

# Sync including dev dependencies (default)
uv sync --all-extras

# Sync without dev dependencies
uv sync --no-dev
```

`uv.lock` is a cross-platform lockfile. Commit it to version control for reproducible builds.

### Running Commands

```bash
# Run a Python script
uv run python script.py

# Run a module
uv run python -m pytest

# Run a project script defined in pyproject.toml
uv run my-cli

# Run pytest directly
uv run pytest -v

# Run with extra environment variables
uv run --env-file .env python main.py
```

`uv run` auto-creates the virtual environment and installs dependencies if needed.

## pip-Compatible Interface

```bash
# Install packages (like pip install)
uv pip install requests flask

# Install from requirements file
uv pip install -r requirements.txt

# Compile locked requirements (like pip-compile)
uv pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt

# Sync environment to requirements (like pip-sync)
uv pip sync requirements.txt

# Uninstall packages
uv pip uninstall requests

# List installed packages
uv pip list

# Show package info
uv pip show requests

# Freeze current environment
uv pip freeze
```

The `uv pip` subcommand is a drop-in replacement for pip and pip-tools with 10-100x better performance.

## Virtual Environments

```bash
# Create a virtual environment (10-100x faster than python -m venv)
uv venv

# Create with a specific Python version
uv venv --python 3.12

# Create with a custom path
uv venv .venv-test

# Activate (same as standard venv)
source .venv/bin/activate    # Linux/macOS
.venv\Scripts\activate       # Windows
```

## Python Version Management

```bash
# Install a Python version
uv python install 3.12
uv python install 3.11 3.12 3.13

# List available Python versions
uv python list

# List installed Python versions
uv python list --only-installed

# Pin project to a Python version
uv python pin 3.12

# Uninstall a Python version
uv python uninstall 3.11
```

## Tool Management

```bash
# Install a CLI tool globally (like pipx)
uv tool install ruff
uv tool install black

# Run a tool without installing (like npx)
uv tool run black .
uv tool run ruff check .

# Shorthand for uv tool run
uvx ruff check .
uvx black --check .
uvx pytest

# List installed tools
uv tool list

# Uninstall a tool
uv tool uninstall ruff

# Upgrade a tool
uv tool upgrade ruff
```

## Building and Publishing

```bash
# Build sdist and wheel
uv build

# Build only wheel
uv build --wheel

# Publish to PyPI
uv publish

# Publish to a custom index
uv publish --index-url https://upload.pypi.org/legacy/ --token $PYPI_TOKEN
```

## pyproject.toml Configuration

```toml
[project]
name = "my-project"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "My Python project"
requires-python = ">= 3.11"
dependencies = [
    "requests>=2.31",
    "httpx>=0.27",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=8.0",
    "ruff>=0.6",
]

[project.scripts]
my-cli = "my_project.cli:main"

[tool.uv]
dev-dependencies = [
    "pytest>=8.0",
    "mypy>=1.10",
]

[tool.uv.workspace]
members = ["packages/*"]

[tool.uv.sources]
my-lib = { path = "../my-lib" }
```

## Workspaces (Monorepos)

```toml
# Root pyproject.toml
[tool.uv.workspace]
members = ["packages/*", "apps/*"]
```

```bash
# Sync entire workspace
uv sync

# Run in a specific package
uv run --package my-lib pytest
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `UV_CACHE_DIR` | Override cache directory (default: platform-specific) |
| `UV_PYTHON` | Default Python interpreter path |
| `UV_INDEX_URL` | Override default package index (default: PyPI) |
| `UV_EXTRA_INDEX_URL` | Additional package indexes |
| `UV_SYSTEM_PYTHON` | Set to `1` to allow system Python usage |
| `UV_LINK_MODE` | Set to `copy` or `hardlink` for install strategy |
| `UV_NO_CACHE` | Disable caching entirely |
| `UV_NATIVE_TLS` | Use system TLS instead of rustls |

## Resolution Strategies

```bash
# Lowest compatible versions (useful for library testing)
uv lock --resolution lowest

# Highest compatible versions (default)
uv lock --resolution highest

# Override a dependency resolution
uv add --override "numpy<2.0"
```

## Performance

- **10-100x faster** than pip for installs and resolution
- **Global cache** eliminates redundant downloads — packages are cached once and linked into each project
- **Written in Rust** — no Python startup overhead, parallel resolution and installation
- **Lazy resolution** — only resolves what has changed
- **Cross-platform lockfile** — `uv.lock` works on Linux, macOS, and Windows

sections.title: Quick start
sections.body: Install uv with `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh`. Create a project with `uv init my-project`. Add dependencies with `uv add requests`. Run code with `uv run python main.py`. uv auto-creates the virtual environment and lockfile. Sync an existing project with `uv sync`.

sections.title: Project management
sections.body: uv manages the full project lifecycle: `uv init` scaffolds pyproject.toml, `uv add` and `uv remove` manage dependencies, `uv lock` resolves and writes uv.lock, `uv sync` ensures the environment matches the lockfile, and `uv run` executes commands within the project environment. Dev dependencies use `uv add --dev`. Workspaces support monorepos via `[tool.uv.workspace]` in pyproject.toml.

sections.title: pip-compatible interface
sections.body: The `uv pip` subcommand is a drop-in replacement for pip and pip-tools. Use `uv pip install` for ad-hoc installs, `uv pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt` to compile locked requirements, and `uv pip sync requirements.txt` to sync an environment. This is useful when migrating existing projects or working with requirements.txt-based workflows.

sections.title: Python version management
sections.body: uv can install and manage Python versions directly, replacing pyenv. Use `uv python install 3.12` to install, `uv python list` to see available versions, and `uv python pin 3.12` to set the project default. Use `uv venv --python 3.12` to create a venv with a specific Python version. The `uvx` shorthand runs CLI tools without permanent installation, like npx.

sections.title: Performance and caching
sections.body: uv is 10-100x faster than pip, written in Rust with parallel resolution and installation. A global cache stores downloaded packages once and links them into project environments, eliminating redundant downloads. The cache can be configured via UV_CACHE_DIR. The uv.lock lockfile is cross-platform and deterministic. Use `--resolution lowest` for library compatibility testing.

links.context: Standard pip installer — uv replaces and extends pip functionality
Virtual environments — uv venv is 10-100x faster than python -m venv
Poetry for dependency management — uv is a faster alternative
Run pytest and other test tools via uv run

related.note: uv replaces pip with a faster, more capable package installer
uv replaces poetry for dependency management, lockfiles, and publishing
uv venv creates virtual environments 10-100x faster than python -m venv

scripts.name: Initialize uv Project
scripts.description: Create a new uv project, add dependencies, and sync the environment
scripts.inline:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

PROJECT_NAME="${PROJECT_NAME:-my-project}"
DEPS="${DEPENDENCIES:-}"

echo "Creating project: $PROJECT_NAME"
uv init "$PROJECT_NAME"
cd "$PROJECT_NAME"

if [ -n "$DEPS" ]; then
  echo "Adding dependencies: $DEPS"
  IFS=',' read -ra DEP_ARRAY <<< "$DEPS"
  for dep in "${DEP_ARRAY[@]}"; do
    dep=$(echo "$dep" | xargs)
    if [ -n "$dep" ]; then
      uv add "$dep"
    fi
  done
fi

echo "Adding dev dependencies: pytest, ruff"
uv add --dev pytest ruff

echo "Syncing environment..."
uv sync

PYTHON_VERSION=$(uv run python --version 2>&1)
echo ""
echo "Project created: $(pwd)"
echo "Python version: $PYTHON_VERSION"
echo "Lockfile: uv.lock"
echo "Virtual env: .venv/"
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  cd $PROJECT_NAME"
echo "  uv run python hello.py"
echo "  uv add <package>"

PROJECT_NAME.description: Name for the new project directory

DEPENDENCIES.description: Comma-separated list of dependencies to add (e.g., requests,httpx,pydantic)

dependencies.description: List of package dependencies to install

dev_dependencies.description: List of dev dependencies to install

project_path.description: Absolute path to the created project

python_version.description: Python version used in the project

author: dojo-community
